Defendants Steve Sisler, Jenny Sanders' Amended Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-31-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ujvala Singh, Esq. (415-593-8240) of Carlson Calladine & Peterson LLP for Plaintiff.
Michael Johnson, Esq. (415-438-6648) of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court states the issue whether there is a distinction in agreeing to act as a general contractor or being a general contractor. Defendant argues the amended cross complaint. The Court states the issues on the JOP and MSJ.
Plaintiff argues difference between express and implied contract.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Steve Sisler, Jenny Sanders' Motion For Summary Judgment

Defendants Steve Sisler and Jenny Sanders' motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendants fail to satisfy their summary judgment burden that plaintiffs Michael and Meehyun Kurtzman cannot prove their malicious prosecution clam. The Kurtzmans'sole cause of action is based on the alleged false allegation that Mr. Kurtzman stated that he would act as the general contractor on the defendants' home improvement project, not on the allegation that Mr. Kurtzman actually acted as the general contractor. Defendants do not present evidence that Mr. Kurtzman stated that he would act as the general contractor. The record on this motion contains evidence to the contracy from Ms. Sanders' deposition and trial testimony and Mr. Sisler's trial testimony. Per Singleton v. Perry (1955) 45 Cal. 2d 489, 497, if any "charges" were made maliciously and without probable casue, a malicious prosecution action lies, even tough others were not made maliciously or without probable cause. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR 176, email:geales@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ujvala Singh, Esq. (415-593-8240) of Carlson Calladine & Peterson LLP for Plaintiff.
Michael Johnson, Esq. (415-438-6648) of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court states the issue whether there is a distinction in agreeing to act as a general contractor or being a general contractor. Defendant argues the amended cross complaint. The Court states the issues on the JOP and MSJ.
Plaintiff argues difference between express and implied contract.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Steve Sisler, Jenny Sanders' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Defendants Steve Sisler and Jenny Sanders' motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. The sole cause of action alleged by plaintiffs Michael and Meehyun Kurtzman is based on the alleged false allegation that Mr. Kurtzman stated that he would act as the general contractor on the defendants' home improvement project. That allegation served as the basis for several of the causes of action asserted in the first amended cross-complaint in the underlying action. Judge Robertson's ruling on the directed verdict motion in the underlying case did not address the merits of that allegation and thus his ruling on that motion does not preclude the assertion in this case that defendants lacked probable cause for their allegation in the underlying case that Mr. Kurtzman stated that he would act as the general contractor.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Notice Of Defendants' Special Motion To Strike And Special Motion To Strike Complaint Under Anti-Slapp Statute
Off Calendar Dec-01-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: J. Michael Matthews of Carlson, Calladine & Peterson LLP telephone number 415-391-3911 for and with Plaintiffs Michael Kurtzman and Meehyun Kurtzman.
Linda Lynch of Lynch and Shupe, LLP telephone number 650-579-5950 for Defendants Steve Sisler and Jenny Sanders.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Defendants Steve Sisler and Jenny Sanders' anti-SLAPP motion to strike the sole cause of action in the complaint for malicious prosecution is denied. Defendants satisfied their burden under the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis that the malicious prosecution claim arises from activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. However, plaintiffs Michael and Meehyun Kurtzman satisfied their burden under the second prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis that there is minimal merit to their malicious prosecution claim. Plaintiffs' claim for malicious prosecution is based on plaintiffs' contention that the defendants knowingly and falsely asserted as the bases for several of their cross-claims in the underlying action that plaintiffs agreed to act as a general contractor for defendants' deck and landscaping construction project. Plaintiffs submitted admissible evidence in opposing this motion that shows that plaintiffs never agreed to act as a general contractor for defendants' project and defendants knew that plaintiffs never agreed to act as a general contractor. These facts are sufficient to show that plaintiffs have enough evidence to establish that the defendants filed their cross-claims without probable cause. Plaintiffs submitted admissible evidence in opposing this motion that shows that defendants' initiated and maintained their cross-claims for the purpose of seeking to obtain litigation leverage against the plaintiffs by creating the threat that defendants might lose their architectural licenses. These facts are sufficient to show that plaintiffs have enough evidence to establish that the defendants filed their cross-claims with malice. Defendants' arguments that plaintiffs' claim for malicious prosecution is barred as a matter of law by the litigation privilege and the advice of counsel defense lack merit. The litigation privilege does not apply to a claim for malicious prosecution. The advice of counsel defense is not available where a party knowingly makes material misstatements to her counsel. Plaintiffs have presented admissible evidence in opposing this motion that Ms. Erhardt's advice to defendants that led to the filing of defendants' cross-claims was based on the defendants' knowingly false statements that plaintiffs agreed to act as a general contractor for defendants' project. Plaintiffs' request for fees and costs in opposing the motion is denied since the court finds, per CCP 425.16(c)(1), that the motion is neither frivolous nor solely intended to cause unnecessary delay. Order signed in open Court.
